seoTitle: How to Make Rows Thinner in a Canva Table
metaDescription: Learn how to adjust row height in a Canva table to create cleaner, more compact tables for reports and presentations.
intro: A table with rows that are too tall can waste space and make your design feel unbalanced. This tutorial covers how to adjust row height in a Canva table so your data displays cleanly in presentations, reports, or social graphics.

steps.heading: Review Canva's table feature
steps.body: Get familiar with how tables work in Canva before making adjustments to row height.

steps.heading: Make rows thinner
steps.body: Select the rows you want to adjust and reduce their height to create a more compact table.

steps.heading: Avoid common design mistakes
steps.body: Make sure text still fits comfortably within the thinner rows so nothing gets cut off or looks cramped.

steps.heading: Apply this in business contexts
steps.body: Use thinner rows for tables in presentations or marketing materials where space and readability both matter.

faq.question: Will making rows thinner cut off my table text?
faq.answer: It can if the row becomes too small for the font size, so check readability after adjusting.

faq.question: Can I adjust individual rows instead of the whole table at once?
faq.answer: Yes, Canva typically lets you resize individual rows rather than forcing uniform height across the table.

faq.question: Does adjusting row height affect column width too?
faq.answer: No, row height and column width are generally independent settings you can adjust separately.

faq.question: Is this table feature available on Canva's free plan?
faq.answer: Basic table functionality is generally available on the free plan, though some advanced styling may require a paid subscription.
